questions.question: List two sensors commonly used in a smart home system and state their purpose.
expectedPoints: PIR/Motion Sensor: Detects movement for security lights or alarms.
Temperature/Humidity Sensor (e.g., DHT22): Monitors ambient conditions for climate control.

questions.question: Explain, with a diagram, the architecture of an IoT-based precision agriculture system for automated irrigation. Discuss the role of each layer.
expectedPoints: Perception Layer: Sensors (soil moisture, temp) in the field collect data.
Network Layer: Gateway (e.g., Raspberry Pi) aggregates data from sensors using protocols like LoRaWAN and connects to the cloud via Wi-Fi/cellular.
Application Layer: Cloud platform analyzes data; if moisture is low, it sends a command back to an actuator (water valve) to irrigate.
A clear ASCII or labeled block diagram should accompany the explanation.
Mention the challenge of powering remote field sensors.
